pi_items.verified_value: Fluo-N2DL-HeLa - real dataset from Cell Tracking Challenge with HeLa cells stably expressing H2b-GFP from Mitocheck Consortium

pi_items.source: Image metadata (DPI: 39379.84 = 25400/0.645), Cell Tracking Challenge technical specifications (https://celltrackingchallenge.net/2d-datasets/), and setup_task.sh specification
Cell Tracking Challenge technical specifications (https://celltrackingchallenge.net/2d-datasets/) and setup_task.sh (frame_interval=1800 seconds = 30 minutes)
Task metadata and verifier.py line 122. Conservative lower bound; documented cancer cell migration velocities range from 7-86 µm/hr, making 1.0 an allowance for slow-moving cells

Task metadata and verifier.py line 122. Upper bound; while most documented cancer cells migrate at 7-86 µm/hr, some cell lines exceed 100 µm/hr (e.g., NCI-H446 lung cancer at 85.7±6.7 µm/h, some exceeding 100 µm/h)

Task metadata and verifier.py line 124. This range falls within documented cancer cell migration ranges: cancer cells display 7-86 µm/h depending on cell line. HeLa cells (cervical cancer) would be expected within this physiologically realistic range
Task metadata and verifier.py line 103. Standard minimum for statistical validity of velocity measurements
Task metadata, setup_task.sh line 7, and export_result.sh line 4

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real experimental data from the Cell Tracking Challenge Fluo-N2DL-HeLa dataset (HeLa cells expressing H2b-GFP, publicly available from https://data.celltrackingchallenge.net/training-datasets/Fluo-N2DL-HeLa.zip). The dataset has calibrated imaging specifications: pixel size 0.645 µm/pixel (verified from image DPI metadata of 39379.84 DPI) and time interval 30 minutes between frames. The biological velocity ranges are physiologically plausible: minimum 1.0 µm/hr is conservative, maximum 150.0 µm/hr accommodates outliers, and the expected mean range of 5.0-80.0 µm/hr falls within documented cancer cell migration velocities (7-86 µm/h range). The task requires minimum 3 cell tracks for statistical validity.
